description:
You are acting as a storage administrator who must make sure that recently-rotated backup archives have not been corrupted while freeing up disk space.  
A “reference” checksum file already exists. Your assignment is to:

1.  Create a fresh manifest of every regular file that is currently inside
        /home/user/backups

    • The manifest MUST be written to
        /home/user/backups/manifest-current.log   

    • It MUST contain exactly one line per file, sorted
      lexicographically by the relative path of the file (the relative path is the path **below** /home/user/backups).  
    • Every line must have the following four pipe-separated columns (no extra spaces):  

          relative_path|size_bytes|sha256sum|timestamp_iso8601  

      – relative_path   : e.g. “daily/db.sql.gz”  
      – size_bytes      : *decimal* byte size returned by stat ‑c%s  
      – sha256sum       : 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters  
      – timestamp_iso8601 : UTC creation time of the file, in the exact
                            form  YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  
    • Example line (do not hard-code):  
          daily/db.sql.gz|3|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad|2023-11-18T09:32:11Z  

    Hint: a single pipeline that finds the files, runs sha256sum **in parallel** (e.g. GNU parallel, xargs -P, or anything similar) and finally formats the output is acceptable.  Absolute paths must NOT appear in the manifest.

2.  Compare the new manifest against
        /home/user/backups/reference-manifest.log  
    and create a human-readable verification report called  

        /home/user/backups/integrity-check-report.log  

    The report must contain one line for every entry that exists in the reference file (even if the corresponding data file is missing now).  The line format is strictly:

          relative_path|expected_sha256|actual_sha256|status

    • expected_sha256 is the 64-char value from the reference file.  
    • actual_sha256  is the hash you have just calculated **or** the literal string “MISSING” if the data file does not exist.  
    • status must be either MATCH, MISMATCH, or MISSING (exact capitals).  

    Lines have to come out in the same order as in the reference file.

3.  On standard output, print a concise summary that looks exactly like:

        === Integrity-check summary ===
        Total files compared : N
        Matches              : X
        Mismatches           : Y
        Missing              : Z
        ==============================

    with the numbers substituted, then exit.

IMPORTANT RULES THE AUTOMATED TEST WILL CHECK
• File paths and names must match those given above **exactly**.  
• Both log files must use Unix line endings (LF) and **must finish with a trailing newline**.  
• The integrity-check-report.log must contain three lines (one for each file listed in the reference manifest) and its status column must read:  
      MATCH for daily/db.sql.gz  
      MATCH for daily/files.tar.gz  
      MISMATCH for weekly/full.tar.gz  
  (The hashes in the middle columns can be any valid 64-char hex strings but the correct status keywords are mandatory.)  
• The summary that you echo to the terminal must match the template above character-for-character except for the integers.

You do not have root privileges; work entirely under /home/user.  If you need temporary files, create them under /home/user/tmp and delete them when done.

When everything is correct, the automated checker will parse
/home/user/backups/integrity-check-report.log and your console summary to grade the exercise.
truth:
Initial on-disk resources (before the agent starts):

1. Directory structure (permissions 755 everywhere):
   /home/user/backups/
   ├── daily/
   │   ├── db.sql.gz           (3-byte file, contents exactly: abc)
   │   └── files.tar.gz        (3-byte file, contents exactly: abc)
   └── weekly/
       └── full.tar.gz         (3-byte file, contents exactly: def)

2. Reference checksum file:
/home/user/backups/reference-manifest.log  
(Mode 644, ends with exactly three newline-terminated lines)

Contents:
daily/db.sql.gz|3|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad
daily/files.tar.gz|3|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad
weekly/full.tar.gz|3|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad

(Note: The two “daily” files currently match their expected hash; the “weekly” file does NOT.)

3. Verification criteria the grader will enforce after the agent finishes:

a) /home/user/backups/manifest-current.log must exist and contain three newline-terminated lines, each having four ‘|’-separated columns as described.

b) /home/user/backups/integrity-check-report.log must exist and consist of:

daily/db.sql.gz|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad|<any 64-hex>|MATCH
daily/files.tar.gz|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad|<any 64-hex>|MATCH
weekly/full.tar.gz|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad|<any 64-hex but NOT the same as expected>|MISMATCH

(all three lines must be present in exactly this order, and file ends with a newline).

c) The agent’s terminal output must include the exact seven-line ASCII summary block with numbers:
   Total files compared : 3
   Matches              : 2
   Mismatches           : 1
   Missing              : 0

d) No extra files are expected to remain except, optionally, temporary ones inside /home/user/tmp which the grader will ignore.
